aft: adverb. At, near, or towards the stern of a vessel (with the frame of reference within the vessel).
ail: verb. To cause to suffer; to trouble, afflict. (Now chiefly in interrogative or indefinite constructions.)
ait: noun. An island in a river, especially the River Thames in England.
alif: noun. The first letter of the Arabic alphabet: ا (ʔalif).
alit: verb. simple past and past participle of alight
alt: noun. Of a voice or instrument, high pitch; especially, the octave above the top line of the treble stave.
faith: noun. A trust or confidence in the intentions or abilities of a person, object, or ideal from prior empirical evidence.
faithful: adjective. Loyal; adhering firmly to person or cause.
fat: adjective. Carrying more fat than usual on one's body; plump; not lean or thin.
fault: noun. Culpability; the responsibility for a blameworthy event.
fiat: noun. An arbitrary or authoritative command or order to do something; an effectual decree.
fifth: adjective. The ordinal form of the number five.
fil: noun. A Nordic dairy product, similar to yogurt, but using different bacteria which give a different taste and texture.
filth: noun. Dirt; foul matter; that which soils or defiles.
fitful: adjective. Characterized by fits (convulsions or seizures).
flat: adjective. Having no variations in height.
flit: noun. A fluttering or darting movement.
haft: noun. The handle of a tool or weapon.
hail: noun. Ball s or pieces of ice falling as precipitation, often in connection with a thunderstorm.
half: noun. One of two usually roughly equal parts into which anything may be divided, or considered as divided.
halt: verb. To limp; move with a limping gait.
hat: noun. A covering for the head, often in the approximate form of a cone, dome or cylinder closed at its top end, and sometimes having a brim
hatful: noun. The amount that will fit into a hat.
haul: verb. To transport by drawing or pulling, as with horses or oxen, or a motor vehicle.
hilt: noun. The handle of a sword, consisting of grip, guard, and pommel, designed to facilitate use of the blade and afford protection to the
huff: noun. A heavy breath; a grunt or sigh.
hula: noun. A form of chant and dance, which was developed in the Hawaiian Islands by the Polynesians who originally settled there.
hut: noun. A small, simple one- storey dwelling or shelter, often with just one room, and generally built of readily available local materials.
lat: noun. A staff, particularly one of an Indian kind.
lath: noun. A thin, narrow strip, fastened to the rafters, studs, or floor beams of a building, for the purpose of supporting a covering of
lathi: noun. A heavy stick or club, usually used by policemen.
lit: verb. simple past and past participle of light (“illuminate; start a fire; etc”)
luff: noun. The vertical edge of a sail that is closest to the direction of the wind.
tail: noun. The caudal appendage of an animal that is attached to their posterior and near the anus or cloaca.
tau: noun. The letter Τ / τ in the Greek alphabet; being the nineteenth letter of the Classical and Modern Greek, and the twenty-first letter of
thulia: noun. The rare earth thulium oxide, Tm 2 O 3.
tiff: noun. A (generally small) argument or disagreement; a petty quarrel, a spat.
tufa: noun. Calcareous lime deposited by precipitation from a body of water, such as a hot spring.
tuff: noun. A light porous rock, now especially a rock composed of compacted volcanic ash varying in size from fine sand to coarse gravel.
tui: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tupuri.
ahu: noun. The goitered gazelle, Gazella subgutturosa.
aitu: noun. A ghost or spirit, often malevolent, in Polynesian mythology.
alf: noun. A supernatural being similar to an elf; one of the Dǫkkálfar or Ljósalfar.
auf: symbol. ISO 639-5 language code for Arawan languages.
auh: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Aushi.
aul: noun. A village encampment in the Caucasus, Central Asia or the Southern Urals.
flaith: noun. An Irish lord, prince, or chief.
hui: noun. A Māori social gathering or assembly.
huia: noun. A wattlebird († Heteralocha acutirostris), endemic to New Zealand 's North Island but extinct since the early twentieth century.
hutia: noun. Any of the medium-sized rodents of the subfamily Capromyinae, which inhabit the Caribbean islands.
lai: noun. A mostly North European medieval form of lyrical, narrative poem written in octosyllabic couplets that often deals with tales of
lait: verb. To seek; search for; inquire.
lif: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Limbu.
tai: noun. A fish, the Japanese bream.
tua: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Wiarumus.
utai: noun. The singing that accompanies noh (classical Japanese drama).
util: noun. A hypothetical unit measuring satisfaction.
